title: crepis sancta subsp. bifida
intro: Crepis sancta subsp. bifida, also known as the Sacred Hawk's Beard or Rough Hawksbeard, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. Crepis sancta subsp. bifida can be used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
